repo_prior_art.cite: eid_data_fusion_k_source_v1: source-cover succeeds iff target interventional law is identified from k source laws and factor-local invariance clauses

published_prior_art.cite: DahabrehRobertsonPetitoHernanSteingrimsson2023 §3.4/Theorem 3 already identifies target potential outcome means under weaker overlap conditions over the collection of trials; proposal adds an atom-arm iff/necessity framing

repo_prior_art.cite: doc/API.md §3.1 Nonparametric Bounds on Treatment Effects: bounded-outcome Manski-style interval machinery

published_prior_art.cite: Manski/Robins nonparametric bounded-outcome treatment-effect bounds; KlineTamer2018 applies partial identification to selective randomized-trial participation

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_data_fusion_k_source_v1: finite multisource cover/witness theme, though in a more general data-fusion setting

published_prior_art.cite: DahabrehRobertsonPetitoHernanSteingrimsson2023 §3.4 gives the two-trial mild/severe support-fragmentation motivating example; proposal adds a finite missing-edge witness

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The proposal says DahabrehRobertsonPetitoHernanSteingrimsson2023 only gives multi-trial formulas under participation/treatment positivity, but §3.4/Theorem 3 already treats fragmented trial support via weaker overlap over the collection.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The sharp interval is a direct bounded-outcome Manski/Robins rectangle bound applied atomwise to missing potential-outcome means, so the partial-ID kernel is already known.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The closest published prior for the interval is the classical Manski/Robins nonparametric bounded-outcome bounds literature, which is absent from the checklist and bibliography.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The flagship comparison needs a location-level anchor to DahabrehRobertsonPetitoHernanSteingrimsson2023 §3.4/Theorem 3 and must state exactly what remains beyond A4†/A5†.

novelty_flags.label: proposal
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship; the atom-cover certificate is named, but the current contribution is a finite diagnostic/specialization rather than a new flagship-grade frontier unless it is made a strict extension of §3.4 with a nontrivial new regime.

soundness_flags.label: Locked parameters / Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The locked identifying-assumptions line lists the atom-cover certificate C(P) as an assumption, while Conjecture 1 treats C(P) as the iff condition and Theorem 2 studies failure of C(P); reword C(P) as the candidate certificate, not an assumed identifying condition.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The proof route is essentially definition unfold plus rectangularity plus linear optimization over bounded missing means; as a flagship-aspirant sharp-set claim this is a routine Manski-class specialization.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Rewrite the main gap against DahabrehRobertsonPetitoHernanSteingrimsson2023 §3.4/Theorem 3: isolate a strict arm-specific or source-specific regime where their A3/A4†/A5† conditions do not apply but the atom-arm certificate does.
Add Manski/Robins bounded-outcome bounds to Related work and downgrade Theorem 2 to a corollary unless the proposal supplies a genuinely new sharpness argument beyond rectangular missing-cell bounds.

Turn the certificate into a computable frontier with an explicit decision problem and complexity statement, or pair it with an estimator/inference result for the fragmented-support regime to strengthen beyond a finite diagnostic.

recommended_next_step: Revise around Dahabreh 2023 §3.4 as the true closest comparator and either prove a strict new regime beyond it or pivot, because the current kernel is field-tier below the flagship floor.
